title: Baiting Pythium myriotylum from Infested Soil
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bept2","text":"Baiting Pythium from seedlings in infested soil or infected hulls.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":8,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bdpte","text":"P5ARP plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":1,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3rfbq","text":"CMA/PDA+amp plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"15vr5","text":"Soil with Pythium infestation signs","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":10,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9gd65","text":"Seeds of susceptible peanut cultivar; we used PI 378012 from the USDA Germplasm Collection","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4sj7q","text":"Fume hood and tools for sterile plating technique","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hlto","text":"Sieve and beakers for surface sterilization","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sji","text":"RO water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2tsji","text":"Forceps and scalpel/blade","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"65vok","text":"95% ethanol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2t19p","text":"Flame","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ibgn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8v7q","text":"For seedling baiting:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dprfu","text":"4\" pots with catch tray","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5k1h4","text":"greenhouse or adequate outdoor conditions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8hgug","text":"Mature banker plants (Alyssum and Peppers) for IPM","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prep P 5 ARP Plates 1-2 days before plating. Prepare working culture plates (CMA, PDA + amp) up to 1 week before plating.
Check plates after 24-48 h.

Check for "gold coin" oospores at plate edges to indicate Pythium myriotylum . Use CMA for oospore production Use full strength PDA to increase hyphal growth for DNA extraction Use V8 to increase hyphal growth for DNA extraction (untested)
Select appropriate samples with black rotted segments. Rotted segments of peanut seedling suitable for plating
Try with or without 95 % (v/v) ethanol soak for 00:04:00 or rinse in sterile RO water. Use a sieve in a small beaker. Do not use bleach due to Pythium sensitivity. [2]
Plate on P 5 ARP or other media with bacterial inhibition (CMA or PDA + amp) [4].
Hyphal tip transfer using sterile pin to clean plates of CMA (V8 or PDA).
Cut up segments into 1/4 inch (1-2 cm) pieces.
Select whether you want to bait from diseased hulls in soil or from seedlings grown in diseased soil.
Germinate susceptible seedlings by wrapping in RO-dampened paper towels and incubating for 2-4 days at 28 °C in the dark. Check daily to re-wet paper towels as necessary.
Cut up hull segments into 1/4 inch (1-2 cm) pieces.
Blot completely dry on sterile paper towels.
Plate on P 5 ARP or other media with bacterial inhibition (CMA or PDA + amp). Jeffers, S. N., & Martin, S. B..
Check plates after 24-48 h. Pythium growth is quick, with white-cream hyphae.
Do a hyphal tip transfer pf the growing mycelial edge using sterile loop to clean plates of CMA (V8 or PDA).
Check for "gold coin" oospores at plate edges to likely indicate Pythium myriotylum. If so, continue to DNA sequencing protocol.
Plant germinated seeds in infested soil, in triplicate, in 4" diameter pots with a drip tray. Maintain greenhouse at 70 % humidity with temperatures between 24 °C and 30 °C .
Water to maintain a level of 1-2" in drip tray for the first 2 days, and then to maintain soil moisture without excessive water until seedlings reach 4-6" in height.
Harvest seedlings. Rinse plants thoroughly in RO water to remove soil particles.
Try with or without 95 % ethanol soak for 00:04:00 . Do not use bleach due to Pythium sensitivity. Stanghellini, M. E., & Kronland, W. C.. Garren, K. H..
Rinse in sterile RO water for 00:04:00 using a sieve in a small beaker.
At 4 DAP, trim to 1 seed/pot, or carefully repot in separate pots.
Grow for 15-20 days and check for rot symptoms. Brown rot will start at ~4-6 DAP, wilt at ~6-18 DAP. Those exposed to higher temperatures may be more susceptible to rot.
Blot dry on sterile paper towels.
creator: Nimalka Weerasuriya
